Washington LIBA program

En Español

Save money with Low-Income Bill Assistance (LIBA) monthly bill discounts. Eligibility is based on your household income and your household size. Program enrollment is open to all Washington residential customers who meet income guidelines. Participants are enrolled for a one-year period. Customers on a fixed income are enrolled for a two-year period.

Immediate help needed? Customers can contact agency partners to request a hardship grant when experiencing a situation that may require immediate financial support. Find contact information for local agencies below.

See if you qualify:

Income guidelines for LIBA

To qualify, a customer’s gross (before taxes) household income, inclusive of deductions in alignment with the Low- Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP), does not exceed the higher of 80% of Area Median Income (AMI) or 200% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL), adjusted for household size.

Monthly Income Eligibility Upper Limits*

80% AMI

Annual Income Eligibility Upper Limits*

Household Size 200% FPL 80% AMI
Columbia
County		 80% AMI
Garfield
County		 80% AMI
Walla Walla
County		 80% AMI
Yakima
County
1 $31,300 $57,250 $54,350 $57,300 $54,350
2 $42,300 $65,400 $62,100 $65,500 $62,100
3 $53,300 $73,600 $69,850 $73,700 $69,850
4 $64,300 $81,750 $77,600 $81,850 $77,600
5 $75,300 $88,300 $83,850 $88,400 $83,850
6 $86,300 $94,850 $90,050 $94,950 $90,050
7 $97,300 $101,400 $96,250 $101,500 $96,250
8 $108,300 $107,950 $102,450 $108,050 $102,450
9 $119,300 $114,450 $108,650 $114,600 $108,650
10 $130,300 $121,000 $114,850 $121,150 $114,850
11 $141,300 $127,550 $121,100 $127,700 $121,100
12 $152,300 $134,100 $127,300 $134,250 $127,300
*Household combined income at or below 200% FPL or 80% AMI, whichever is greater. Qualifying income refers to income before taxes from all sources, both taxable and nontaxable and inclusive of deductions.

List of household income sources

Income used to qualify includes (but not limited to):

Alimony

Annuities

Child Support

Disability Compensation

Dividends

Earned Wages/Salary

Foster Care Payment

Interest

Military Pay

Pensions

Rental Income

Retirement

Royalties

Self-employment income (after expenses)

Social Security

Social Security Disability (SSDI)

Supplemental Security Income (SSI)

TANF

Unemployment

Workers’ Compensation

*Qualifying income refers to total gross annual income from all sources, both taxable and nontaxable.

Income not used to qualify includes:

  • Energy assistance
  • Income for people in high school or under 18
  • Income tax refunds and credits
  • One-time cash gifts
  • Reverse mortgages
  • Housing subsidies paid directly to the landlord
  • Utility allowances
  • WIC benefits

Frequently asked questions & guidelines for applying

What is LIBA?

The Low Income Bill Assistance program provides a bill discount applied to a customer’s monthly net bill.

To qualify, a customer’s gross household income, inclusive of deductions, does not exceed the higher of 80% of Area Median Income (AMI) or 200% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL), adjusted for household size.

Qualifying customers will be placed into one of six qualifying levels.

How do I qualify for LIBA?

The program is available to all income qualified households including property owners or tenants that are Pacific Power customers. Qualifying customers will be placed into one of six qualifying levels. 

Customers may self-certify their household income to qualify for a specific bill discount level. Local community action agencies (see list of agencies further below on this page) may also certify customers for the appropriate bill discount level.

How is the discount applied?

The LIBA discount applies only to bills associated with the customer’s permanent primary residence and only to new charges billed after enrollment. Eligible customers will receive the bill discount within one billing cycle of receipt of their application or re-certification.

How often do I need to enroll?

Re-enrollment will be required every year, or every two years for customers on a fixed income.

How is eligibility confirmed?

On a monthly basis the company will randomly select a percentage of participating self-certified customers from each level to provide post-enrollment verification of their income. Failure to provide documentation will result in removal from the program after 60 days from the time of first notification.

What if my status changes?

A customer may contact the utility or agency (see list of agencies further below on this page) to update their enrollment if their eligibility status changes before their enrollment period ends. Updated eligibility will be treated as re-enrollment.

What if I'm removed from the program?

If the customer is removed from the program, the company will not seek to recover any benefits already provided to the customer, nor will the company provide benefits to the customer during the time the program was cancelled for failure to re-enroll or provide post-enrollment verification.

Applying and renewing for the program is quick and easy. Simply complete an application. There is no paperwork or proof of income required to apply. You may complete an application online, by traditional mail, over the phone with Customer Care at toll-free at 1-888-221-7070 or contact your local community action agency to apply.

If you meet these guidelines, please apply for LIBA:

APPLY NOW

WA LIBA Print Application

You may complete and mail the attached application directly to Pacific Power.

WA LIBA Print Application - for Agency

You may complete the attached application and provide it to your local community action agency for processing.

Local agencies

You may contact your local agency to apply for LIBA and other available resources:

  • Blue Mountain Action Council (Columbia, Garfield, Walla Walla Counties) 509-529-4980
  • Northwest Community Action Center (Yakima County, south of Union Gap) 509-865-7630 or email LIBA@yvfwc.org
  • Opportunities Industrialization Center (OIC) of Washington (Yakima County, including Union Gap and communities north of Union Gap) 509-955-7100
